title: Elymus alienus
taxonomy: Elymus alienus is classified as a species of grass within the genus Elymus, which is a part of the family Poaceae (true grasses). It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Triticeae. Elymus alienus is further categorized as a member of the section Elymus, which is characterized by its spikelets and glumes. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally agreed upon as a member of the above-mentioned categories. Elymus alienus is closely related to other species within the Elymus genus, which are also classified as wild ryegrasses.
uses: Elymus alienus is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various medicinal applications. Traditionally, Elymus alienus was used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. Research has also suggested potential uses in treating various health conditions, including antimicrobial and antifungal properties. However, more studies are needed to confirm the efficacy of Elymus alienus in these areas. While some traditional uses have been reported, modern medicine has not widely adopted Elymus alienus as a treatment option.
benefits: Elymus alienus is a species of grass that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits. It is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, Elymus alienus may have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it potentially useful in wound healing and treating infections. Some studies suggest that the plant may also have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, which could help protect against cell damage and support immune function. However, more research is needed to fully understand the health benefits and potential applications of Elymus alienus.

preparations: Elymus alienus, also known as alien wheatgrass, is a plant used in traditional medicine to make herbal preparations. Its leaves are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures.
